_meta.description: PRC's targeted import bans on Taiwanese goods. Direction is implicit: all entries are direction='prc_imports_from_tw' with status='banned'. Compiled from GACC announcements and contemporary news reporting; HS codes are best-effort 8-digit (PRC system) — many GACC suspensions are at the exporter-registration level rather than the HS-code level, so the `notes` field carries that context.
_meta.review_cadence: Update when news scrape surfaces new GACC import suspensions

bans.notes: GACC cited pest (mealybug) contamination. 91% of TW pineapple exports went to PRC pre-ban. Widely framed as political coercion.
97% of TW sugar apple exports went to PRC pre-ban. GACC cited mealybug.
98% of TW wax apple exports went to PRC pre-ban. Same GACC notice as sugar apples.
GACC cited oxytetracycline residue and prohibited drug use. 90% of TW grouper exports went to PRC pre-ban.
Same GACC notice as live grouper.
Post-Pelosi visit retaliation. GACC suspended imports of fresh citrus from Taiwan citing pest/pathogen detection.
Post-Pelosi visit retaliation. Suspended alongside chilled white scallops and citrus fruits.

bans.product_zh: 糕餅及焙烤食品（特定廠商）
bans.notes: Exporter-level suspension: GACC suspended import registrations from 35 named Taiwanese biscuit/pastry exporters. Approx. 2,000 of TW's 3,200 GACC-registered food product registrations were suspended in this period — a much broader action than the HS-code-level bans listed here.
